summary.title: Ship mainline.sh SEO category pages
summary.what: Switched the website canonical domain to mainline.sh, added the first category-definition and use-case pages, moved comparison pages to the new /compare URL structure, added a CLI install page, refreshed multilingual content, and deployed the static Astro site to Vercel production.
summary.why: The user wanted mainline.sh to define the new category around Git-native memory for coding agents, repo memory for AI coding agents, and engineering intent memory, with canonical SEO assets and multilingual pages in place immediately.
summary.user_goal: Use mainline.sh as the primary domain and implement the prioritized SEO structure for category ownership rather than broad AI traffic.

decisions.chose: Use https://mainline.sh as the Astro site fallback, canonical URL, robots sitemap host, OG host, and Vercel production alias.
decisions.rationale: The user bought mainline.sh and wanted one primary domain to avoid duplicate content across future domains.

decisions.chose: Keep English as the default language and extend the existing Chinese and Spanish static route model to the new SEO pages.
decisions.rationale: The site already used /zh/ and /es/ with shared Astro components; reusing that model keeps the multilingual surface static, indexable, and simple.

decisions.chose: Create reusable SeoPage and InstallPage components backed by src/seoPages.ts.
decisions.rationale: The new pages share structure but need different definitions, examples, related links, and localized copy.

decisions.chose: Move comparison routes to /compare/rag/, /compare/session-memory/, and /compare/pr-descriptions/ while preserving old /comparisons/... paths as redirects.
decisions.rationale: The user requested the new URL structure, and redirects preserve old links without exposing duplicate canonical content.

decisions.chose: Add a hand-authored public/sitemap.xml while leaving @astrojs/sitemap enabled for generated sitemap-index.xml.
decisions.rationale: The user specifically requested /sitemap.xml; Astro's integration emits sitemap-index.xml by default, so the public sitemap gives Search Console the exact URL while the generated sitemap remains harmless.

decisions.chose: Make /install/ an HTML install page and keep the shell command pointed at the GitHub Release installer.
decisions.rationale: There is no dedicated shell-script endpoint at mainline.sh/install yet, so claiming curl https://mainline.sh/install | bash would be misleading.

rejected.reason: The SEO strategy requires one canonical source; future secondary domains should redirect.
The user explicitly prioritized category-definition terms over generic AI traffic.
Static localized routes are simpler, indexable, and consistent with the current Astro site.

acknowledged_constraints.note: Website install copy says init installs config, Git refs, the Mainline skill, and repo-local hooks; it does not say init writes AGENTS.md.
Positioning stays on durable intent and repo memory; comparison pages explicitly distinguish Mainline from session recording.
The tagline remains punchy, while title, description, lede, compare pages, and use-case pages describe Mainline as context surfaced before edits.

review_notes: The rebase integrated remote README.md updates that were already on origin/main; the SEO implementation itself is in the website routes, components, config, and public assets.
The static public/sitemap.xml is the Search Console target and should be updated when new canonical SEO pages are added.
Hero copy still uses a strong 'Stop AI agents...' line, but the surrounding metadata and lede explain the mechanism as pre-edit repo memory rather than hard enforcement.

architectural_claims: mainline.sh is the canonical production domain for the static Astro website.
English, Chinese, and Spanish SEO pages are built as static Astro routes with hreflang alternates.
Old comparison URLs are redirect-only and no longer canonical content routes.
behavioral_changes: Homepage title, description, H1, canonical, OG image URL, nav links, footer links, and contact mail now target mainline.sh positioning.
New indexable pages exist for repo memory, engineering intent memory, AI PR review, install, RAG comparison, session-memory comparison, and PR-description comparison.
llms.txt, favicon.ico, og-image.png, and sitemap.xml are served from public assets.
Vercel production deploy aliases the build to https://mainline.sh.
security_implications: No analytics, forms, cookies, or user data collection were added.
The install page presents the existing GitHub Release installer and checksum path instead of a new shell endpoint.
migration_notes: Redirects map /comparisons/mainline-vs-rag/ to /compare/rag/ and /comparisons/mainline-vs-session-memory/ to /compare/session-memory/ for en, zh, and es.
If usemainline.dev is added later, it should redirect to mainline.sh rather than serving duplicate content.

resolves_followups.rationale: The production domain plan was superseded by the user buying mainline.sh; the site was switched, deployed, and aliased to https://mainline.sh.
